comparisons.title: Save processed files for later analysis
comparisons.note: This block writes the processed CSV and trimmed tree that the Python evidence pass later consumes.

comparisons.title: APE plotting basics
comparisons.note: This is a visual exploration block; the current report does not claim figure-equivalence for base `ape` plots.

comparisons.title: APE alternate layouts: cladogram and fan
comparisons.note: These layout variants are tracked as visual surfaces only.

comparisons.title: Unrooted tree demo
comparisons.note: Both sides produce an unrooted representation with the same tip set and 75 tips.

comparisons.title: Phytools tree plotting exploration
comparisons.note: The `phytools::plotTree()` surface is tracked, but no rendered-figure claim is made here.

comparisons.title: Extract clade descended from R node 77
comparisons.note: The descendant tip set matches exactly when compared by stable taxon signature rather than recycled node labels.

comparisons.title: Rotate-nodes teaching demo
comparisons.note: The child-order rotation results match the R `rotateNodes` tip order for both the single-node and all-node variants.

comparisons.title: Ggtree tree-visualization exploration
comparisons.note: These `ggtree` examples are tracked as visual surfaces only.

comparisons.title: Tip-order alignment for joining data to the tree
comparisons.note: The aligned species order matches the trimmed tree tip order.

comparisons.title: APE tip overlay with longevity
comparisons.note: This is a rendered trait-overlay surface and is tracked separately from ordering correctness.

comparisons.title: Treeio node mapping and joined tree-data object
comparisons.note: Representative node ids align and the joined object size is consistent with the 75-taxon dataset.

comparisons.title: Joined ggtree trait plotting
comparisons.note: Joined-data `ggtree` figures are tracked here as visual outputs only.

comparisons.title: Random simulation scenarios
comparisons.note: For credibility, the report freezes these R-generated simulation inputs with `set.seed(1)` and reuses the resulting artifacts on both sides.

comparisons.title: Random trait plotting surfaces
comparisons.note: These are visual teaching plots and are tracked separately from the simulation inputs and signal fits.

comparisons.title: Random-data lambda fits
comparisons.note: The report checks the explicit random-data fit calls and extends the implied checks to the other generated examples.

comparisons.title: Primate longevity histogram and tip-size plot
comparisons.note: These are visual inspection surfaces only.

comparisons.title: Primate longevity vector assembly
comparisons.note: The longevity vector is aligned to the trimmed tree tip order.

comparisons.title: Primate longevity lambda fit
comparisons.note: The `bijux-phylogenetics` lambda estimate is within a small numerical tolerance of the R fit.

comparisons.title: Lambda-zero visual tree comparison
comparisons.note: The side-by-side real-tree versus lambda=0 plots are tracked as visual outputs only.

comparisons.title: Lambda-zero covariance and likelihood-ratio test
comparisons.note: The covariance surface and lambda-vs-zero test agree within numerical tolerance.

comparisons.title: Continuous ancestral point estimates
comparisons.note: Clade-aligned ancestral point estimates match to floating-point noise.

comparisons.title: Continuous ancestral 95% intervals
comparisons.note: The Brownian/PIC confidence-interval surface now matches the R reference to floating-point noise.

comparisons.title: Assemble ancestral table and node mapping
comparisons.note: The ancestral table assembly is consistent: 75 tip rows plus 74 internal rows on both sides.

comparisons.title: Ancestral colored tree plot
comparisons.note: The ancestral-state branch-color figure is tracked as a visual rendering surface only.

comparisons.title: Bonobo/Gibbon MRCA estimate
comparisons.note: The MRCA clade and the ancestral point estimate match.

comparisons.title: How many times lifespan increased across primates
comparisons.note: The branch-wise increase counts match the direct R reference path.

comparisons.title: Save final analysis workspace
comparisons.note: The lecture script saves an `.RData` workspace; this report saves explicit machine-readable evidence artifacts instead.
